CLAIM OF EXEMPTION PACKET FROM MERCED SHERIFF-CORONER RECEIVED

LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION OF JESSELYNN L.LABELLE. DEFENDANT JUDGMENT DEBTOR LABELLE'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS GRANTED. THE LEVYING OFFICER IS DIRECTED TO RELEASE ANY FUNDS HELD TO THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR. THE LEVYING OFFICER IS FURTHER ORDERED TO WITHHOLD $100 PER PAY PERIOD ($200 PER MONTH) TO SERVICE THE CREDITOR'S JUDGMENT.(AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES/TRANSCRIPT) THE COURT WILL PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)
RETURN OF EXECUTION FOR MONEY ISSUED TO SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY, RETURNED AND FILED, PARTIALLY SATISFIED IN THE SUM OF $564.90 AS TO DEFENDANT LABELLE, JESSELYNN L.

calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Feb-28-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Roger Jackson, Esq. 707-318-2994 for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION OF JESSELYNN L.LABELLE.

Defendant judgment debtor LaBelle's claim of exemption is granted. The levying officer is directed to release any funds held to the judgment debtor. The levying officer is further ordered to withhold $100 per pay period ($200 per month) to service the creditor's judgment.

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

The Court will prepare an order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
